featured.title: New FortiClient EMS flaw exploited in attacks, emergency patch released
featured.summary: Fortinet has released an emergency weekend security update for a new critical FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) vulnerability that is actively exploited in attacks. [...]
featured.suggestedTitle: Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw Actively Exploited, Emergency Patch Released
featured.suggestedSummary: Fortinet has issued an emergency patch for a critical vulnerability in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS), identified as CVE-2026-35616. This flaw is actively being exploited in the wild, posing a significant risk to enterprise networks using this software. The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to unauthorized access and data breaches. Security teams should prioritize applying the patch to mitigate this threat.

featured.title: $285 Million Drift Hack Traced to Six-Month DPRK Social Engineering Operation
featured.summary:
Drift has revealed that the April 1, 2026, attack that led to the theft of $285 million was the culmination of a months-long targeted and meticulously planned social engineering operation undertaken by the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) that began in the fall of 2025.

featured.reason: Significant financial impact from APT attack
featured.suggestedTitle: DPRK Social Engineering Operation Behind $285 Million Drift Hack
featured.suggestedSummary: A sophisticated North Korean social engineering operation, conducted over six months, culminated in the theft of $285 million from Drift. The attack highlights the persistent threat posed by nation-state actors using social engineering to breach financial platforms. This incident underscores the importance of robust security awareness training and multi-layered security controls to defend against advanced persistent threats (APTs).

quickNews.title: Traffic violation scams switch to QR codes in new phishing texts
quickNews.summary: Scammers are sending fake "Notice of Default" traffic violation text messages impersonating state courts across the U.S., pressuring recipients to scan a QR code that leads to a phishing site demanding a $6.99 payment while stealing personal and financial information. [...]

quickNews.title: Hackers exploit React2Shell in automated credential theft campaign
quickNews.summary: Hackers are running a large-scale campaign to steal credentials in an automated way after exploiting React2Shell (CVE-2025-55182) in vulnerable Next.js apps. [...]

quickNews.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
quickNews.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent implant.
